# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF PURPOSE: What's Your Message?*

- id: 35843860
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/35843860

---

##    6\. Read This Before You Do THE DRILL DOWN METHOD 

# **PART I. ARE YOU READY FOR ALL THIS "MESSAGE" BUSINESS?**

Ok, let me ask you two questions.

The first one is:

## Why do you make art?

If you answered:

1\. "I make art for fun."

OR

2\. "I make art for arts sake."

Then you might still be in self-discovery mode right now. Why overload that part of the journey by trying to attach a message to it? 

Eventually, a burning sense of purpose does start to take over your work. A time when making "pretty things", or "making hot beats" is no longer enough... 

Until that passion rises, don't force it.

_But if you DO feel that fire in your belly, then you're in the right place._

Now tell me...

## Who do you make your art for?

If you answered

1\. "Everybody."

OR

2\. "I make art for myself, and if someone else likes it then great."

Again, maybe you should hold off on "finding your message"?

A message only comes in handy when you're ready to communicating an artistic vision to "your people."

If you believe that you're making your art for "everybody"... Then you definitely DON'T want your art to have a message.

Why?

When you have a message you run the daily risk of offending one of those "everybody's" in our hypersensitive culture.

And if it's a really POWERFUL message... Damn, you might even "get cancelled".

Mainstream audiences -- the "everybody's" that many people want to appeal to -- they don't like to be challenged by a "unique artistic vision". The masses prefer the cozy comfort zone of what they already know. They prefer the work of craftspeople and technicians. They are late-adopters to artists.

Hold off on this whole "message business" until you're ready to ruffle some feathers, ok? 

And if you make art "for yourself"... Then you definitely don't need to worry about communicating anything to anyone. You're in "Me, myself and I" mode. You don't need a message. All good.

## But for everybody else... 

If you're still reading then you're one of the rare ones who have come to peace with this: 

**ART = CREATIVE SELF EXPRESSION IN SERVICE OF UPLIFTING A PARTICULAR COMMUNITY**

**That's what Lady Gaga does. That's what Frida Khalo does. That's what Rupi Kaur does. That's what ALL legends do.**

And this whole "finding your message" journey is about:

  1. Identifying your community
  2. and defining the artistic vision you want to communicate to them.



_Ok. Glad we got that out of the way._

# **PART II. DON'T EXPECT A TROPHY AT THE END**

If you think you're gonna do all the exercises in 'The Book Of Purpose' and that at the end of it all, a little ticker tape will spit out your message... And everyone will cheer. Bad news. It's way more lonely than that. 

Purpose is like love: It's generated by passion.

## Nothing happens to you "at the end". Everything that's going to happen to you will happen throughout the process.

More bad news: Nothing here will generate that passion for you.

More good news: The Drill Down Method will bring out everything that's already there, but been hiding.

Searching for your message is a process. A journey. Just like making art is.

And even though I split this experience into 7 days... Understand that it may take longer or shorter for you.

They key is to only attempt this when you can give it your full attention. Don't rush through. Never gloss over anything. I mean, come on... There's a reason it's called The Drill Down Method. You're supposed to go deep. 

You're trying to hit a nerve.

If you get stuck somewhere and you find you can't answer a fundamental question. Don't move forward. Stay in the mud.

> _"It's time for you to answer the questions you've been avoiding. It's time you find the big juicy answers that fire you the fuck up. Find some solitude-- Bring a bag of Doritos and some tea. Lock yourself in that room until you uncover something new. The first answers will be shallow. Allow them to be. Allow for the possibility that you may not like ANY of the answers right away. Or that they may make you rethink your whole direction. Yes, find a place of solitude-- Bring some hummus, crackers and a glass of white wine. And if the answer doesn't come right away... Stare outside your window. Don't stop early because you're bored, like you've done so many times in the past. Let the question linger this time. In mid-air. It'll be uncomfortable at first. You've trained your mind to sweep all uncomfortable feelings under the rug. But, I'm telling you... Have the courage to leave the questions out in the open for a little longer... Go to sleep with the unanswered questions. Wake up with them. Stay unsettled for a few days. Have confidence in the process. Keep it at the forefront of your mind, and let it submerge into your unconscious. That's how you solve it... You'll be astonished to see what appears. How do I know that something will happen? Because The mind hates a vacuum. And if you don't settle for the first thing that jumps out at you... the mind will use every resource to find the answers you're looking for. I promise you. -- Father Bronques_

Everything else in your life is instant gratification. That's the culture these days. But this process requires you to end the habit of staying on the surface of things.

_If you want the truth, that is._

Be ok with sitting with an unanswered question for many nights if that's what it takes.

"Keep the window open."

Hey.

You're trying to reveal your artistic vision. 

We're trying to get you to put it into words the reason your art exists-- So your audience can be riveted by it.

Isn't that end result worth the highest level of introspection?

  


# **PART III. YOU GET WHAT YOU GIVE**

The Drill Down Method will only be as powerful as your partner is willing to go down the rabbit hole with you. 

The Drill Down Method will only be as powerful as HOW MUCH YOU'RE WILLING TO "LET GO" AND REVEAL.

The Drill Down Method is like therapy.

If you hold back... Nothing will come out of it.

If your partner is afraid to call you on your bullshit... Same. Nothing will come out.

It's a lot like art.

Or like life.

You get what you give.

  


# **PART IV. FOCUS ON THE JOURNEY.**

I heard from two students this year who thought their Drill Down was a failure.

They were so focused on "I didn't find my message"-- that they totally missed what they HAD found.

_Take Isabella, for example._

She didn't "find her message". But she discovered her Polaris Star.

_In her words:_

**_Dear Father Bronques,  _**

**_Before The Drill Down Method, I had pretty much decided on being a painter. But around DAY 2 of Drill Down, I noticed that MUSIC kept popping up over and over again. I quickly realized that if I didn't follow my music passion, I would seriously regret it. So I made a choice to switch paths even though it feels like I'm starting a whole new journey._**

**_Those five days showed me things I was unaware of. It made me realize that I wasn't struggling with WHAT I wanted to say after all... I had to figure out HOW I wanted to say it. I know that I don't have THEE message right yet, but now that I'm following my true passion, I feel like everything I'm doing is leading to it anyways. I see it coming out in what I sing and write about already.  _**

**_I appreciate all that you do. Thank you for taking the time to read. I'm open to any suggestions if you think there's something I'm not seeing. Have a beautiful rest of your day!  _**

Not at all, Isabelle... You're seeing everything you need to see.

When you get a major epiphany, don't be afraid to stop and go explore it before you continue.

Don't feel guilty about that. 

## Your journey is not supposed to be linear.

Come back here later when needing to find your message hits you in the face again (if your current epiphany doesn't lead you there all by itself).

The other example I wanted to share with you is Mayra's experience.

_Here is our back and forth:_

**_Dear Father Bronques,_**

**_I tried to do the Drill Down Method again (2nd time). I still don "t quite feel like I've arrived at my message. Lately, I've been beating up on myself and getting frustrated.  _**

**_FATHER BRONQUES: Mayra, let's investigate... How far did you get in The Drill Down?  _**

**_MAYRA: First time we got to Day 3, the second time to Day 5_**

** _FATHER BRONQUES: You can't get to Day 5 without revealing SOMETHING. What did you get so far?  _**

**_MAYRA: "Everyone is (message excluded for privacy). The world requires us to (omitted). Rejecting the boxes and (omitted). I want to make music that is (omitted).  _**

**_FATHER BRONQUES: Mayra, WTF? "Everyone is (omitted)" is already a sick little contrarian slogan. Everything else you wrote is already a great foundation to create a brand identity around. It's so YOU. I mean... Can it be fine-tuned? Of course. Do you still need to figure out your audience and how to tie it all into the music you're making? Yeah, no doubt. But THIS is more than enough to build an online presence that's cohesive and aligned with purpose.  _**

**_MAYRA: Damn, you're right. I had a look through my insta feed and you really can't tell that I am about any of these things. And they are all truly me. You're the first person ever to encourage me to bet on myself. It's scary, but I know it has to be done now. I honestly thought these messages weren't "good enough". As I'm saying it out loud, though, I realize how silly that sounds. I'm starting to understand that this is really just a process:_**

  * **_Figuring out what works._**
  * **_Adapting._**
  * **_Trying something new._**
  * **_Getting feedback and chipping away._**



**_*a few days later, I heard from Mayra again...*_**

 **_MAYRA: I started recording vocals for the first time yesterday. No words yet, just sounds, harmonies, etc. It's a whole different feeling now. I also started sending out emails to my email list of 56 people too :) Everything already feels more "right". Thank you for your words._**

Don't focus on finding a "perfect message".

Focus instead on the epiphanies that you get everyday.

_Those epiphanies will tell you where you need to go next._

  


# **PART V. IF YOU DON'T ACT ON YOUR MESSAGE, YOU'VE WASTED YOUR TIME**

I don't care what beautiful "message sentence," you write down at the end of the 7 days... If your whole demeanor doesn't reflect a newfound clarity and purpose... If you're not propelled into action, then you've wasted your time. 

Don't start Drill Down if you don't think you have the energy to "move different" afterwards.

Dear student,

When you REALLY find your message...

Food tastes different.

You start to question what type of people you've been hanging out with.

You don't want to waste time anymore. Every second is precious.

You have a burning purpose. You dream about it.

When you look at your Instagram feed, you immediately see everything you need to do to make it more YOU.

You make your website a billboard for your artistic vision.

Every newsletter you send to your list reeks of someone burning with forward momentum.

Even your personal emails are fired up. You might even find yourself adding a new "signature" at the bottom to express "the new you".

You're on a mission.

You have more to say than you can possibly share, so you start needing to stagger your online output.

You struggle with NOT making every conversation at dinner about your passion.

But the best part is this:

When people ask you about your art-- the first words out of your mouth is about WHY you make your art not about how good it is. And because of this, even people outside of your community get excited-- before they even have a chance to experience it.

So if you still think you still want to "go there"... 

Allow 'The Drill Down Method' to give you a few epiphanies or reinforce the convictions you were on the fence about.

BUT PLEASE--

While you're doing it... Don't go through the motions.

Don't gloss over questions. Don't skip hard to answer ones. 

And don't go into it thinking that something is going to "happen to you".

No one's bringing you a fortune cookie at the end with your message printed out for you to follow.

If you DO have an artistic vision... This process will reveal it. 

And it will give you the fortitude to pursue it.

But don't wait to apply it.

Don't dare let yourself go back to "business as usual" after this intense process reveals your truth!

Act on it right away.

Before the outside forces convince you that mediocrity and fitting in is the best path again.

_Only ACTION can sustain even the strongest epiphany._

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

After reading this it may not be fair of me to do this. initially I thought I could do it with my wife or close friend, but I probably would want a stranger so I could be more honest without consequence to an existing relationship. Tempted to peek, I saw a warning for me not to do so as I would sour the experience. I have a passion to create, but it's more engineering than artistic like I've stated. Would that be fair for a partner? I think I may have to come back to this book at a later date.

I haven't started the drill down yet and yet the beginning of this filled in another missing link I have bèen frustrated when applying in the past for commissions...I've been told by an eminent that although my application was excellent my work was years ahead of its time. ( I read it as they were behind the times..but guess their audience was) I've been approached to work with communities but rarely for my own work but to support others. I've applied for micro commissions taking up to 2 days to do the application but then rejected. the artists selected were safe mainstream. I stopped doing it as it wasn't working 'smart'. I thought art was supposed to challenge.decided I must go rogue..but I realised why reading this chapter. I've been chasing the wrong audience. I expected mainstream to want to be challenged.. that they would be excited to push boundaries. THEY DONT!!

Dear Father Bronques, I have quite a big issue at least for me. For reasons due to my daily job, that I definitely need because I am a single mother,I can't show my face or voice in media. I work for a Pubblic Service and it's against the rules because I already represent the Service with my presence. I have a strong message on women empowerment but all the influencers here in Italy show themselves. I am not allowed. I would ask if you could have some suggestions even for my case 😅. Thank you so much in advance for your course and all the intriguing and usefull stuff packed inside the mistery school. 🌷

Erik Paul Howard

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/35843860/comments/16328467)

“Art = Creative self expression in service of uplifting a particular community… that’s what ALL legends do.” So simple. So (willfully) concealed. Unapologetic service to that community who deserves to not have our contributions diluted with everybody sauce (which is really about our own comfort in the generated responses and doesn't work ANYway). Thank you for red pill all day every day!

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

"We're trying to get you to put it into words the reason your art exists-- So your audience can be riveted by it. Isn't that end result worth the highest level of introspection?"...... this was such a beautiful statement for this chapter.
